title: Ocean Currents
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Ocean currents are (0) like vast rivers flowing through the sea, moving enormous quantities of water around the planet. These currents play a crucial role in regulating Earth's climate and supporting marine ecosystems.||Surface currents are driven primarily (9) by wind patterns and the Earth's rotation. The Gulf Stream, for example, carries warm water (10) from the tropical Atlantic to northern Europe, making the climate there much milder (11) than it would otherwise be. Without this current, countries like Britain would have winters similar to those of Canada.||Deep ocean currents, (12) which are part of what scientists call the 'global conveyor belt', are driven by differences in water temperature and salinity. Cold, salty water is denser and sinks, creating (13) a circulation pattern that moves water between ocean basins. This deep circulation can take centuries to complete (14) a full cycle, meaning that some water in the deep ocean hasn't been at the surface for hundreds of years.||Ocean currents transport nutrients, affecting (15) where fish populations thrive. They also redistribute heat around the planet, influencing weather patterns and regional climates. Climate change is affecting ocean currents, and scientists are concerned that changes to (16) these systems could have dramatic consequences for global weather patterns and marine ecosystems in the coming decades.

example.text: Ocean currents are (0) vast rivers flowing through the sea.

questions.explanation: Voz pasiva: 'are driven by' indica el agente que impulsa las corrientes.
Expresión 'carries water from X to Y' indica origen y destino.
Pronombre relativo que se refiere a las corrientes profundas.
Adverbio relativo de lugar: 'affecting where fish populations thrive'.
Demostrativo 'these systems' se refiere a las corrientes oceánicas.
